action.reasoning: Observation: The training script failed with a RuntimeError because `mat1 and mat2 shapes cannot be multiplied (16x1348 and 3140x256)`. Analysis: The issue is that SB3 automatically transposes image observations from `(H, W, C)` to `(C, H, W)` if the `dtype` is `np.uint8` and it has 3 channels. This caused our `(7, 7, 3)` image to become `(3, 7, 7)`, which messed up the CNN input shape and resulted in a flattened size of 1344 instead of 3136. Decision: I will fix this by changing the `dtype` of the image observation to `np.int32` in `wrapper.py` (so SB3 doesn't treat it as an image to transpose) and also add a safety transpose check in `model.py`. I will set the total timesteps to 10.5M to ensure it finishes safely within the remaining 1.66 hours.
